---
title: "Writing"
---

### Papers Under Review

* Ryan P. Larson, Sarah Shannon, Aaron Sojourner, and Chris Uggen. "Felony History and Change in U.S. Employment Rates." Resubmit with minor revisions at *Social Science Research*. 

* Amber Hamilton, Doug Hartmann, and Ryan P. Larson. “Assessing and Extending Colorblind Racism Theory Using National Survey Data.” Revise & resubmit at *Sociology of Race and Ethnicity*.

* Veronica Horowitz, Ryan Larson, Victoria Piehowski, Allison Nobles, and Josh Page. “No Contact: The Institutionalization
of Criminal Domestic Violence Protector Orders.” Under review at *Social & Legal Studies*.


### Peer-Reviewed Articles

* Rob Stewart, Bieanna Watters, Veronica Horowitz, Ryan Larson, Brian Sargent, and Christopher Uggen.2021. “Native Americans and Monetary Sanctions. Forthcoming in *The Russell Sage Foundation Journal of the Social Sciences*.

* Jeanie Santaularia, Ryan P. Larson, and Christopher Uggen. 2021. [“Criminal Punishment, Child Abuse, and Violent
Injury in Minnesota.”](pubs/santaularia_etal_21.pdf) *Injury Epidemiology*, 8(11): 1-10.

* Stewart, Evan, Penny Edgell, Sarah-Catherine Billups, and Ryan Larson. 2019. [“The Stakes of Symbolic Boundaries.”](pubs/stewart_etal_19.pdf)
*The Sociological Quarterly*, 61(2): 309-333.

* Ann Meier, Ben Hartmann, and Ryan Larson. 2018. [“A Quarter Century of Youth Activity Participation: Inequalities by Race, Class, Gender and Age?”](pubs/meier_etal_18.pdf). *Journal of Youth and Adolescence* 47(6): 1299-1316.

* Larson, Ryan P. and Andrew Lindner. 2018. [“Professionalization Through Atrrition?: An Event History Analysis of Mortalities in Citizen Journalism.”](pubs/larson_lindner_18.pdf) *Information, Communication, and Society* 21(5): 746-760.

* Lindner, Andrew and Ryan P. Larson. 2017. [“The Expansion and Contraction of the Journalistic Field and American Online Citizen Journalism, 2000-2012”](pubs/lindner_larson_17.pdf). *Poetics* 64: 40-52.

* Hartmann, Doug, Paul Croll, Ryan Larson, Joseph Gerteis, and Alex Manning. 2017. [“Colorblindness as Identity: Key Determinants, Relations to Ideology, and Implications for Attitudes about Race and Policy”](pubs/hartmann_etal_17.pdf).
*Sociological Perspectives* 60(5): 866-888.

* Hawkins, Daniel, Andrew Lindner, Ryan Larson, and Johnathan Santos. 2015. [“Overrating Bruins, Underrating Badgers: Media, Prestige, and College Basketball Rankings.”](pubs/hawkins_etal_15.pdf) *Journal of Sports Management and Commercialization*
6(3): 11-26.

### Technical Reports

* Uggen, Christopher, Ryan Larson, Sarah Shannon, and Arleth Pulido-Nava. 2020. ["Locked Out 2020: Estimates of People Denied Voting Rights Due to a Felony Conviction"](https://www.sentencingproject.org/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/Locked-Out-2020.pdf) Washington, D.C.: The Sentencing Project.

* Uggen, Christopher, Ryan Larson, and Sarah Shannon. 2016. ["6 Million Lost Voters: State-Level Estimates of Felony Disenfranchisement, 2016"](https://www.sentencingproject.org/wp-content/uploads/2016/10/6-Million-Lost-Voters.pdf) Washington, D.C.: The Sentencing Project.

### Book Chapters

* Larson, Ryan P.. 2019. [“Felon Disenfranchisement.”](pubs/larson_19.pdf) *Voting and Political Representation in America: Issues and Trends*, edited by Mark P. Jones. Santa Barbara, CA: ABC-CLIO.

* Andrew Lindner and Ryan P. Larson. 2018. [“Regression Analysis.”](pubs/lindner_larson_18.pdf) *Encyclopedia of Sociology*, edited by George
Ritzer. Oxford: Wiley-Blackwell.

* Larson, Ryan P. and Chris Uggen. 2017. [“Felon Disenfranchisement.”](pubs/uggen_larson_17.pdf) *Encyclopedia of Corrections*, edited by
Kent R. Kerley. Oxford: Wiley-Blackwell.

### Articles

* Uggen, Christopher and Ryan P. Larson. 2017. [“Crime Trends in the GSS.”](pubs/uggen_larson_contexts_17.pdf) *Contexts* Fall 2017.

* Lindner, Andrew and Ryan P. Larson. 2016. [“The Year English Soccer Wasn’t For Sale.”](pubs/lindner_larson_contexts_16.pdf) Contexts Fall 2016.

* A collection of contributions to [*The Society Pages*](https://thesocietypages.org/) can be found [here](https://thesocietypages.org/search/ryan%20larson/).
